The New Content Package Wizard

To create a new content package, click New on the File menu. Choose Content Package and click OK. This starts the New Content Package Wizard.

  1. After displaying the welcome dialog box, the New Content Package Wizard presents a dialog box asking for the following information.
    2. Content Package Name
    Specifies the name of the content package. This name is used as the name of the XML project file for the submission. It cannot contain any folder or path information. The maximum length of the content package name is 38 characters. Valid characters for the content package name are the characters a–z, A–Z, the numerals 0–9, and the characters !, #, $, &, ', (, ), -, @, [, ], ^, _, `, {, }, and ~. You do not need to provide a file name extension.
    Project Directory
    Specifies the path to the directory containing the project file.
    Title ID
    Contains the 8-digit hexadecimal title identifier that you receive from Microsoft.
    Publisher Offering ID
    Specifies an optional identifier that you can associate with the package. You can use the Publisher Offering ID to differentiate this submission from other content packages related to the title. This ID must be specified as a 7-digit hexadecimal number.
    Activation Date
    Contains the date from which you intend the submission to be available for download. Because the activation date does not apply if your content is distributed on DVD, content packages distributed on DVD can be assigned any date you want.
    Dashboard Icon Bitmap
    Specifies the name of the file containing the bitmap that the Dashboard displays after the user installs the content on the console. The bitmap should be 64×64 pixels in a BMP file.

  2. In the next dialog box, the New Content Package Wizard asks for a set of flags that specifies whether the package being created is compatible with all versions of the title. Clicking No enables you to specify a set of flags indicating which versions of the title the content package can be used with.

    For example, some countries have certain restrictions about what can be included in games. If the package being created cannot be used in those countries, you can define flags that indicate the incompatibility. You would then create a separate package with modified content that is usable in those countries. You would use these flags to indicate that the modified content is for use with the version of the game that is marketed in those specific conuntries.

    Specify each of these flags as an 8-digit hexadecimal value. A value of zero is not valid. Setting these flags to all ones (that is, 0xFFFFFFFF) indicates that the content package is compatible with all versions of your title.

  3. The New Content Package Wizard next displays a dialog box asking you to select the languages your title supports. Select the check boxes next to the names of the languages your title supports.
  4. The Display Settings dialog box enables you to enter localized strings and an icon bitmap for each languge that your title supports. You can enter strings for both the Xbox Dashboard and the Xbox Content Download Manager.

    Note    The Download Manager Icon Bitmap must be stored in a BMP file that is exactly 168×128 pixels in size.

    Note    The Content Download Manager string can contain the HTML tags <b>, </b>, and <br>. For more information, see Downloader Enum BLOB Tool (DlEnum)

  5. The New Content Package Wizard next displays a dialog box stating that you have finished inputting date for your project. Click Finish to create the XML project file.

There is a known issue in the XLAST New Content Package Wizard in that, on Windows 2000, it improperly disallows some valid icon bitmap files. For this issue, use either of the following workarounds.

Workaround 1

  1. Install GDI+ from the Microsoft downloads site.
  2. Place GDIPlus.dll in the %XDK%\xbox\bin directory.

Workaround 2

  1. In the Wizard, specify the file %WINDIR%\Zaptotec.bmp instead of your bitmap file.
  2. After the Wizard completes, use the tree-view control to change Zaptotec.bmp to your bitmap.
